description: You are organizing software project documentation in TiddlyWiki for a project called ProjectAlpha. Establish a hierarchical Table of Contents structure by creating a root tiddler titled 'ProjectAlpha Documentation' (including a brief project description) and three section tiddlers—'Architecture', 'API Reference', and 'Getting Started'—tagged with the root title. Organize the 12 existing documentation tiddlers into this hierarchy: tag 'Database Schema Design', 'Microservices Overview', and 'Authentication Flow' with 'Architecture'; tag 'REST API Endpoints', 'GraphQL Queries', 'Error Codes Reference', and 'WebSocket Events' with 'API Reference'; and tag 'Installation Guide', 'Configuration Options', 'Development Environment Setup', 'Deployment Checklist', and 'Troubleshooting Common Issues' with 'Getting Started'. Create a 'Documentation Index' tiddler that uses a Table of Contents macro (such as <<toc-selective-expandable "ProjectAlpha Documentation">>) to display the full hierarchy. The wiki is accessible in Firefox at http://localhost:8080/ and auto-saves to the server.
